attributes.content:
{{letter_date}}

{{sr_number}}

{{case_number}}

{{mis_number}}

{{client_fullname}}

{{client_street_address}}

{{client_city}}, {{client_province}} {{client_postal_code}}

Dear {{client_fullname}}:

Re: CPP tax exemption on retroactive CPP assigned to the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction

You have recently been approved for Canada Pension Plan Disability (CPPD) and are now receiving these benefits. As the CPPD benefit is a taxable income, you may be required to pay income tax on CPP benefits you receive.

When you completed your CPPD Application Package, you signed the Consent to Deduction and Payment form (ISP1613). In signing this form, you authorized Service Canada to repay the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction any backdated CPPD benefits to prevent duplicate payments. This agreement makes sure that you receive the full benefits to which you are entitled while also preventing an overpayment.

The ministry has now received your backdated CPPD benefits from Service Canada and reviewed your income assistance file. We are pleased to tell you that you have been found eligible for a one time payment for CPP tax exemption.

You have been issued a one-time tax liability adjustment in the amount of ${{payment_amount}}. If you have previously been set up for Electronic funds transfer, (EFT), the amount of ${{payment_amount}} will be deposited into your account. If you were not set up for EFT, please find the enclosed adjustment cheque.

Only clients who are eligible for the CPP tax exemption are eligible for the tax liability adjustment.

Please contact the ministry at the address or phone number listed below if you have any questions.

Phone: 1-877-815-2363, option 3.
Mailing Address: Provincial CPP Program Services
PO Box 9950 Stn Prov Govt
Victoria, BC V8W 9R3

Sincerely,

CPP Program Services

The Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction operates under the authority of the Employment and Assistance Act and Regulations, and the Employment and Assistance for Persons with Disabilities Act and Regulations
Ministry of Social
Development and
Poverty Reduction
Provincial CPP Program
Services
